content.verdict: Grace Hotel Santorini is an adults-only boutique hotel of just 20 rooms in Imerovigli — the highest point of the Santorini caldera rim, often called 'the balcony of Santorini' — with arguably the most dramatic infinity pool view on the island. Smaller and slightly more boutique-feeling than the Canaves properties in Oia, Grace has a design-led contemporary aesthetic layered over the traditional cave architecture, with an exceptional infinity pool that seems to flow directly into the caldera. Its Imerovigli position means you're above the Oia crowds while still having one of the finest caldera panoramas on the island.
content.best_room: The Caldera Suites with private heated plunge pool are the essential honeymoon booking — positioned on the caldera's edge with private terraces and personal plunge pools, they deliver the Santorini dream in its purest form. The Grace Suite is the crown jewel of the property with its private infinity edge pool and 270-degree caldera views. All rooms come with the hotel's signature Molton Brown amenities and the option of an in-room hammam treatment.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Imerovigli & the Caldera Rim
itinerary_7_nights.description: Transfer from the airport and check into your suite as the caldera spreads before you — Imerovigli's elevated position means the view is even more expansive than in Oia. A welcome cocktail at the infinity pool edge, where the pool's water appears to meet the caldera's horizon, sets the tone immediately. The first dinner at Grace's restaurant on the caldera terrace is an event in itself.

itinerary_7_nights.title: The Infinity Pool & Spa
itinerary_7_nights.description: Spend the morning at the extraordinary infinity pool — at 280 meters above the sea, the view combines the caldera, the volcanic islands, and the Aegean in one sweeping panorama. Book a couples' treatment at the spa, which offers traditional Greek therapies using local volcanic minerals and olive oil. Evening drinks at the sunset terrace before returning to your plunge pool.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Walk to Oia & Back
itinerary_7_nights.description: The cliff path from Imerovigli to Oia (about 3.5 km) is one of the most beautiful walks in the Cyclades — a caldera-edge path past windmills, ruins, and the dramatic Skaros Rock. Walk it in the morning before the heat arrives and before the day-trippers fill Oia. Have breakfast in Oia's quieter northern lanes, then walk or taxi back to Grace for a long afternoon by the pool.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Caldera Boat Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Arrange a private sailing trip around the caldera and volcanic islands through Grace's concierge. Visit the sulfurous hot springs at Nea Kameni, swim off the boat in the caldera's remarkably clear water, and stop at the tiny island of Thirasia for a late lunch at a family-run taverna. Return to the hotel at sunset for a glass of Assyrtiko on your plunge pool terrace.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Fira & Akrotiri
itinerary_7_nights.description: Take a taxi to Fira for morning shopping and the excellent Museum of Prehistoric Thera, which houses extraordinary artifacts from the Minoan city buried by Santorini's ancient volcanic eruption. Continue to the Akrotiri archaeological site itself — a remarkably well-preserved Bronze Age city frozen in volcanic ash since 1620 BC. Return to Grace for evening cocktails and a special dinner.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Wellness & Local Flavors
itinerary_7_nights.description: Full morning of spa treatments followed by a cooking class arranged through the concierge, learning traditional Greek recipes — fava from Santorini's own yellow split peas, tomatokeftedes (tomato fritters), and proper Greek salad with the island's extraordinary cherry tomatoes. Picnic the results on your private terrace overlooking the caldera.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell Morning
itinerary_7_nights.description: A final plunge in your private pool at sunrise, breakfast on the caldera terrace, and a leisurely departure that the Grace team will orchestrate with typical care. The hotel's position on the highest point of the caldera rim means your last view of Santorini — as the transfer car descends toward the airport — is of the entire island laid out below you.

caveats: Grace Hotel is on the caldera rim in Imerovigli, not in Oia — the famous Oia sunset village is a 3.5 km walk or short drive away. Couples fixated on Oia's specific location may prefer Canaves.
No beach access — this is a caldera-view, pool-focused hotel. Beaches require a drive to the east coast of the island.
With only 20 rooms, the hotel reaches capacity quickly and has limited restaurant seating — plan dining reservations immediately upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is Grace Hotel Santorini good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Exceptionally so. The combination of adults-only policy, intimate 20-room scale, extraordinary infinity pool caldera views from Imerovigli's highest point, and design-led romantic suites make it one of the finest honeymoon boutiques in Greece. It's slightly more intimate and less branded than some of Oia's more famous properties.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: May, June, and September are ideal for Santorini — warm and sunny without peak August's intense crowds and prices. October is also beautiful, with quieter villages, warm seas, and a golden autumn light. The hotel is open from April through October.

faqs.question: What should we book in advance?
faqs.answer: Book the hotel and specifically a Caldera Suite with plunge pool as far in advance as possible. Pre-arrange catamaran trips and spa treatments through the concierge before arrival. The hotel restaurant books up — reserve for multiple evenings at check-in.

faqs.question: Is it adults-only?
faqs.answer: Yes, Grace Hotel Santorini is adults-only. The intimate scale and caldera-edge setting create an atmosphere that is entirely couple-focused, with no children on the property.

faqs.question: What's the best room type for a honeymoon?
faqs.answer: A Caldera Suite with private plunge pool is the essential booking. The Grace Suite with its private infinity pool is the ultimate luxury option. All bookings should confirm caldera views — the hotel's design positions most rooms toward the caldera, but it's worth confirming at booking.

faqs.question: How do we get there?
faqs.answer: Fly into Santorini (JTR) from Athens (45 minutes) or direct from major European cities. From the airport, Imerovigli is about 15 minutes by private taxi. The hotel can arrange transfers. Note that Santorini's cable car and donkey path connect Fira (a 10-minute drive from Imerovigli) to the Old Port below — an option for arriving by ferry.
